context: Tests whether the agent generates Gherkin .feature files that follow IIKit's mandatory tagging conventions (@TS-XXX, @FR-XXX, @SC-XXX, @US-XXX, priority, test type), covers the majority of success criteria, and includes the required DO NOT MODIFY header on each file.

checklist.name: TS-XXX tags present
checklist.description: Every Scenario has a @TS-XXX tag (sequential numeric ID, e.g. @TS-001, @TS-002) on the line immediately before the Scenario keyword

checklist.name: FR-XXX tags present
checklist.description: Every Scenario has at least one @FR-XXX tag referencing a functional requirement from spec.md

checklist.name: SC-XXX tags present
checklist.description: At least some scenarios include @SC-XXX tags referencing success criteria from spec.md

checklist.name: SC-XXX majority coverage
checklist.description: At least 3 of the 5 SC-XXX success criteria (SC-001 through SC-005) have at least one scenario tagged with them across the generated .feature files. System-level criteria that cannot be expressed as Gherkin scenarios may be excluded.

checklist.name: US-XXX tags present
checklist.description: Each Scenario has a @US-XXX tag referencing the parent user story

checklist.name: Priority tags present
checklist.description: Every Scenario includes a priority tag (@P1, @P2, or @P3) matching the priority of its parent user story

checklist.name: Test type tags present
checklist.description: Every Scenario includes a test-type tag (one of @acceptance, @contract, or @validation)

checklist.name: DO NOT MODIFY header
checklist.description: Each .feature file starts with lines containing 'DO NOT MODIFY SCENARIOS' and instructions to write step definitions and not modify .feature files

checklist.name: Feature-level US tag
checklist.description: Feature-level lines include @US-XXX tags for the parent user story

checklist.name: All acceptance scenarios covered
checklist.description: All 8 Given/When/Then acceptance scenarios from the spec.md are represented as Gherkin Scenarios in the .feature files

checklist.name: TS-XXX uniqueness
checklist.description: All @TS-XXX tags are unique across all .feature files (no two scenarios share the same TS-XXX number)
